Selected work / 2021—Now

Built across the whole signal path.

Projects where software meets the physical world, from smart-home hardware to operational tools and digital platforms.

01 / 06
Latest project · Active

UNIfesb

A native student super-app bringing timetables, X-ica spending, canteen cameras, jobs, sports and faculty information into one private, local-first experience.

SwiftUIWebKitWidgetKitMetal
Open case study
02 / 06
Founder project · Active

Wawe Home

A modular smart-home ecosystem that makes switches, sensors, schedules and automations feel straightforward.

ESP32PCBFlutterAppwrite
Open case study
03 / 06
Exploration · In development

AI Wearable

An exploration of what a personal assistant becomes when it is designed as a discreet, purpose-built object.

AIEmbeddedInteraction
Open project log
04 / 06
Product prototype · 2023

Luggie

A mobile operations tool for resort luggage and transport teams, designed around clear ownership and quick hand-offs.

FlutterOperationsMobile
Open case study
05 / 06
Student platform · 2022

Ssteh

A learning-resource hub made to help high-school students find and share useful material without the usual clutter.

WebEducationCommunity
Open case study
06 / 06
Space data portal · 2021—25

BeyondSpaceNews

A Nuxt-powered hub aggregating spaceflight news, NASA datasets, live ISS positioning and launch data through a resilient server API layer.

Nuxt 3NitroAppwrite
Open case study
Ideas / Field notes

Opinions on where technology should go next.

Notes on the systems I want to understand, build and help move from interesting prototypes into useful infrastructure.

ROB / 001
Robotics8 min

Humanoids are a software problem wearing a hardware body

Capable robots will come from intelligence, redundancy and scale—not perfect hardware alone.

Read article
 EV / 002
Electric mobility9 min

The EV transition will be won by efficiency, not battery size

Better vehicles, ubiquitous charging and honest education matter more than headline capacity.

Read article
 AI / 003
AI agents7 min

AI agents become infrastructure when they can run locally

The useful future is private, affordable and dependable enough to trust with real work.

Read article
 AIR / 004
Autonomous logistics7 min

Drone delivery should become boring infrastructure

For lightweight parcels and groceries, the default journey should be direct and automated.

Read article
Explore all ideas
About / Direction

Curious by default. Serious about systems.

I’m Ivan, a 20-year-old student based in Zadar, currently studying Electronics and Computer Engineering at FESB.

I learn quickly, take ownership and give every task the best of my ability. Then I look for what can be simplified, measured, automated or improved—the engineer’s instinct to understand the system and leave it better than I found it.

Based inZadar, Croatia
Current studyElectronics + Computer Engineering
01

Electric mobility

My clearest long-term direction: the electronics behind electric vehicles—battery systems, power conversion, sensing, embedded control and the engineering that turns energy into motion.

02

Spaceflight systems

Spaceflight combines extreme constraints with ambitious engineering. That fascination inspired BeyondSpaceNews and continues to shape how I think about reliability, telemetry and complex systems.

03

Precision products

I’m drawn to consumer hardware where motors, airflow, power electronics, sensors and industrial design are resolved into products that feel simple in use.

04

Engineering mindset

I move comfortably between hardware and software, learn unfamiliar tools fast and treat every responsibility—large or small—as a chance to make the outcome more reliable and efficient.
